sections.heading: Architecture Overview
sections.content: NEXUS is built as a real-time multi-threaded system combining AI inference, stereo vision, and hardware control. The system follows a modular architecture where six core Python modules communicate via a global CONFIG dictionary and PyQt6 thread-safe signals.

sections.heading: Core Modules Map
rows: Global configuration manager loading and saving settings.json
Main QThread running the full AI inference loop at 30+ FPS
Kalman filter application for smoothing raw YOLO bounding boxes/keypoints
Thread-safe serial comms, rate-limiting (15ms), and auto-reconnect monitor
Pure static mathematical methods for homography, stereo, and triangulation
Asynchronous QThread preventing UI freeze during heavy cv2.stereoCalibrate() computation

sections.heading: Data Flow Pipeline (VisionEngine.run)
steps: Capture: Read stereo frames simultaneously from left and right USB cameras
Rectify: Apply cv2.remap() using pre-computed maps to epipolar-align both frames
Infer: Run YOLO inference on the left frame via TensorRT
Lock: Apply IoU single-target locking to maintain tracking across frames
Extract: Identify shoulder keypoints [5] and [6] to compute the physical aim point
Smooth: Pass the aim point through GhostPredictor (Kalman Filter)
Transform: Convert pixel coordinates to servo angles via Homography matrix (H)
Depth: Compute real-world distance via stereo disparity between L/R cameras
Dispatch: Send P{pan}T{tilt}L{laser}\n commands to the ESP32 via TacticalSerial

sections.heading: Thread Safety & Signal Flow
sections.alertContent: PyQt6 Signal Architecture: The VisionEngine (background QThread) is strictly forbidden from updating the UI directly. Instead, it emits frame_ready(np.ndarray) and telemetry_ready(dict) signals. These cross thread boundaries via Qt.QueuedConnection, safely updating the main GUI thread without race conditions.
